items.title: Everything New in iOS 27 Beta 2
items.summary: Apple released the second beta of iOS 27 today, and as is common with a second beta, there are multiple changes. There are new features and updates to existing features as Apple prepares for a July public beta and a September software...

items.title: Tesla pushes back on Autopilot narrative after fatal Texas crash
items.summary: Whether the Autopilot system was truly active, overridden, or malfunctioning likely won't be resolved until investigators finish combing through the vehicle's data logs.

items.title: Leaker Prosser's default ruling set aside by judge as Apple lawsuit continues
items.summary: A July 2025 lawsuit by Apple against leaker Jon Prosser resulted in a default ruling, but that default has been set aside as Prosser has finally agreed to participate in discovery. Jon Prosser continues to make new videos in spite of...

items.title: Judge agrees to set aside default entered against Jon Prosser in Apple lawsuit
items.summary: Jon Prosser is getting a new chance to respond to Apple's complaint over the leak of iOS 26 and the Liquid Glass revamp. Here are the details.

items.title: Apple confirms AirPort Utility app is going away soon
items.summary: Apple's release notes for iOS 27, iPadOS 27, and macOS 27 reveal that AirPort Utility will soon be removed from the App Store. Here are the details.

items.title: Apple's Camera-Equipped AirPods Ultra: What to Expect
items.summary: Apple is working on AirPods that have cameras for feeding data to Siri . The new earbuds will be Apple's first AI wearable, and current rumors suggest they're going to come out in late 2027. Subscribe to the MacRumors YouTube channel for...

items.title: NHTSA probes fatal Tesla crash into Texas home that killed woman
items.summary: Federal safety regulators are now investigating the fatal Tesla crash in Katy, Texas, where a Model 3 left a residential road, tore through a brick home, and killed a 76-year-old woman inside. The National Highway Traffic Safety...

items.title: Arm servers capture over 45% of data center market revenue - GPU clusters and high-end AI infrastructure fuel a tectonic shift away from x86
items.summary: Arm-based servers accounted for nearly half of server revenue in Q1 2026, challenging x86. But in the coming years, they might catch up unit wise as well.

items.title: Your Google Pixel just got a secret Hi-Fi audio boost with Android 17
items.summary: Android 17 finally bring LHDC Bluetooth codec support to Pixel devices.
